item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents consistently reject the statement from turn 1 through the final turn. Agent A explicitly maintains opposition while addressing Agent B's public safety and abuse-of-power framing. Agent B likewise maintains a rights-first position and allows only narrow exceptions for public health, emergency response, and direct harm. No visible flip or directional drift appears; both agents make limited concessions that collective needs can matter under strict constraints.
